Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Apr-10-2025 Continued To Jun-13-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER CANDLESTICK HEIGHTS COMMUNITY ALLIANCE's Motion For Writ Of Mandate.

The hearing on Petitioner's Motion For Writ Of Mandate is CONTINUED TO June 13, 2025.

Supplemental Briefing:
No later than May 22, 2025, Petitioner is to file a supplemental memorandum of points and authorities identifying what statute or statutes it is requesting writ relief under (e.g. CCP Section 1085, 1094.5), and the specific action Petitioner is challenging under that authority, the applicable legal standard, and how the record supports that relief under that standard. No later than June 2, 2025, Respondent may file an opposition brief. No later than June 6, 2025, Petitioner may file a reply.

Courtesy Copies:
Filing parties must deliver to this department a courtesy copy of any filing within 24 hours of the filing. The failure to timely provide courtesy copies may result in a delay or an order taking the motion off calendar.

Supplemental Brief Administrative Record:
Petitioner must also submit hard copies of the administrative record that is relevant to the single cause of action remaining: violation of the public trust doctrine. The administrative record must be indexed in consecutive volumes not to exceed 300 pages. The cover of each volume must identify the page range.

Petitioner shall forthwith lodge by email addressed to contest email a proposed order with the above text and shall promptly serve the executed order on all parties entitled to notice.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
